title: Lord of the Sabbath
subtitle: The hungry disciples pluck grain in the fields, and the Pharisees murmur

stillness: Be still, and let the noise of the world fall away.
The sabbath was not made for idleness,
but that, resting, you may remember God to be your Maker.
gospel: On a sabbath day the Lord passed through the grainfields.
His disciples, being hungry, began to pluck the ears of corn,
rubbing them in their hands, and eating the grains,
to console the want of their hunger.
But the Pharisees, seeing it, said to him:
Behold, your disciples do what is not lawful to do on the sabbath.
behold: Behold the dishes of the Apostles: the grains of the ears of corn;
for they were doves, and the nature of doves is to eat grains.
They were hungry, partly from poverty,
partly from the pressing throng of the crowds,
for they had no space even to eat.
They did not gather prepared meats, but sought the simplest food,
and in plucking the ears, they consoled their want.
O blessed Apostles, in whom the body asked for its own
only when the spirit had nothing left to do.
consider: Necessity there had no law, nor ought it to have one here.
Necessity makes a thing lawful that otherwise would be unlawful;
and so, the disciples being hungry,
what was not lawful in the Law was made lawful by the necessity of hunger.
The Pharisees were not grieving for any injury to the Law;
they were seeking an occasion for slander,
for they themselves sinned against the Law every day.
He who grieves at another's sin is far perfected in justice;
but no one can be more merciful to another than to himself.
God wills mercy more than sacrifice,
for the works of mercy are more acceptable to God than victims.
The Lord made the sabbath for man,
that in it man might rest and be free for God;
he did not make man for the sabbath.
And so the Son of Man is Lord even of the sabbath.
prayer: Lord Jesus, you are greater than the temple, for you are its Lord;
you are Lord even of the sabbath.
You will the affection of mercy and of pity
more than the ceremonies of the old Law;
for the sacrifice pleasing to God is the salvation of men.
Set me free from terthings to which my heart has clung,
that the chaff be parted from the grain within me,
and so feed the hunger of your Church.

latinJewel.latin: Hostia enim Deo placabilis est salus hominum.
latinJewel.english: For the sacrifice pleasing to God is the salvation of men.
